briefdescription.para: Initialize the scheduler with a task array.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to an array of SYN_Task structs. Each task should already be initialized via syn_task_create().
Number of tasks in the array.

memberdef.argsstring: (SYN_Task *task, const char *name, SYN_TaskFunc func, uint8_t priority, void *user_data)
memberdef.name: syn_task_create

detaileddescription.para: Sets the task to READY state with the protothread reset to the top.

parameterdescription.para: Human-readable name (stored by pointer, not copied).
Optional pointer to task-private data (or NULL).

briefdescription.para: Execute a single task's protothread function.

simplesect.para: The protothread status returned by the task.

detaileddescription.para: Scans all tasks and runs the single highest-priority ready task (lowest priority value first, round-robin among equal priorities).

simplesect.para: true if at least one task is still alive (not DEAD).

detaileddescription.para: Equivalent to while (1) { syn_sched_run(sched); }. This function never returns.

briefdescription.para: Suspend a task. It will be skipped by the scheduler until resumed.
Resume a suspended task, making it eligible to run again.
Restart a task from the beginning of its protothread.

detaileddescription.para: Resets the protothread continuation and sets the task to READY.

briefdescription.para: Get the number of tasks that are still alive in the scheduler.
